1. <Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
2.   <PropertyGroup>
3.     <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
4.   </PropertyGroup>
5. 
6.   <Target Name="GenerateTimestamp" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
7.     <PropertyGroup>
8.       <TimestampFile>$(IntermediateOutputPath)build-timestamp.txt</TimestampFile>
9.     </PropertyGroup>
10.     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(TimestampFile)" Lines="Built at: $([System.DateTime]::Now.ToString())" Overwrite="true" />
11.   </Target>
12.

14.     <Exec Command="git rev-parse HEAD" ConsoleToMsBuild="true" IgnoreExitCode="true">
15.       <Output TaskParameter="ConsoleOutput" PropertyName="GitHash" />
16.     </Exec>
17.     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs"
18.                      Lines="namespace IncrementalBroken { internal static class BuildInfo { public const string GitHash = "$(GitHash)"%3B } }"
19.                      Overwrite="true" />
20.     <ItemGroup>
21.       <Compile Include="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs" />
22.     </ItemGroup>
23.   </Target>
24. </Project>

     </ItemGroup>
   </Target>

data.content:
## Incremental Build Issues Found

This project has **two custom targets** that break incremental builds:

### 1. `GenerateTimestamp` (line 6–11)
- **Problem:** Writes the current time (`DateTime::Now`) to `build-timestamp.txt` on every build. Since neither `Inputs` nor `Outputs` are declared, MSBuild always runs this target. The file changes every time (new timestamp), which can also trigger downstream rebuilds.

### 2. `EmbedGitHash` (line 13–23)
- **Problem:** Missing `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes, so it always re-executes. It runs `git rev-parse HEAD` and writes `git-hash.g.cs` every build. Even when the git hash hasn't changed, the file is overwritten (same content but new modified-time), potentially forcing recompilation.

### Fixes

| Target | Fix |
|--------|-----|
| `GenerateTimestamp` | Add `Inputs`/`Outputs` — or better, remove it entirely since a volatile timestamp inherently defeats incrementality. |
| `EmbedGitHash` | Add `Inputs="$(ProjectDir).git/HEAD;$(ProjectDir).git/refs/heads/**"` and `Outputs="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs"` so it only runs when the commit actually changes. Also use a `WriteOnlyWhenDifferent`-style approach (or a `Condition` comparing existing content) to avoid touching the file when the hash is unchanged. |

The core principle: every custom target needs `Inputs`/`Outputs` so MSBuild can skip it when outputs are already up-to-date relative to inputs.
